perturbation.original_text: Recipient will maintain in confidence and will no t disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Infor mation belonging to and/or related to Society, whet her or not in written form. Recipient agrees that Recipient s hall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient furthe r represents that Recipient exercises at least reason able care to protect its own confidential informati on.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will maintain in confidence and will not disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Information belonging to and/or related to Society, whether or not in written form, UNLESS compelled by law or legal process. Recipient agrees that Recipient shall treat all Confidential Information of the Society with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient further represents that Recipient exercises at least reasonable care to protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by adding an exception that allows disclosure of 'Confidential Information' if 'compelled by law or legal process'. This contradicts the original absolute prohibition on disclosure, introducing ambiguity and legal uncertainty about what constitutes a breach of the agreement. This now creates uncertainty as there is now a valid situation where confidential information can be disclosed. If they are allowed to, is it a breech of contract? Is it not?
perturbation.contradicted_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements co ntained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society fo r which there will be no adequate remedy at law, an d the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief and/ or a decree for specific performance, and such othe r relief as may be proper (including monetary damages if approp riate).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the non-disclosure agreement, stating that disclosure is permitted if 'compelled by law or legal process.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that 'A breach of any of the promises or agreements contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society.' The addition of the exception means that disclosure under legal compulsion would be a breach with no adequate legal remedy. The original text makes the obligations to not disclose absolute; the changed text contradicts this absolute obligation by allowing some disclosures, which then clashes with the 'breach' clause.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient understands that its obligations under Paragraph 2 ("Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations") shall surv ive the termination of any other relationship between the p arties. Upon termination of any relationship betwe en the parties, Recipient will promptly deliver to the Soc iety, without retaining any copies, all documents a nd other materials furnished to Recipient by the Society.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient understands that its obligations under Paragraph 2 ("Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations") shall survive the termination of any other relationship between the parties for a period of ONE year. Upon termination of any relationship between the parties, Recipient will promptly deliver to the Society, without retaining any copies, all documents and other materials furnished to Recipient by the Society.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text limits the survival of nondisclosure obligations to 'ONE year', contradicting the original clause implying that those obligations survive indefinitely. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding how long the Recipient must keep the information confidential after the end of the relationship, potentially leading to disputes about breaches of contract after one year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will maintain in confidence and will no t disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Infor mation belonging to and/or related to Society, whet her or not in written form. Recipient agrees that Recipient s hall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient furthe r represents that Recipient exercises at least reason able care to protect its own confidential informati on.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the recipient's nondisclosure obligations to ONE year, while the contradicted text states that the recipient will maintain confidentiality without any time restriction. This is a direct contradiction because the contradicted text implies indefinite confidentiality, which is not compatible with the modified one-year limitation. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia and by the laws of the St ate of Victoria; as such laws are applied to agreem ents entered into and to be performed entirely within Au stralia.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia, BUT in case of conflict the laws of the State of New South Wales will govern; as such laws are applied to agreements entered into and to be performed entirely within Australia.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that while the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia govern, in case of conflict, the laws of the State of New South Wales will govern. This contradicts the initial statement indicating exclusive governance by the laws of Victoria and creates confusion about which jurisdiction's laws apply in a dispute. This can influence on how a party is governed and what should be done.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia, BUT in case of conflict the laws of the State of New South Wales will govern; as such laws are applied to agreem ents entered into and to be performed entirely within Au stralia.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it initially states that the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia govern the agreement. However, it then introduces a conflicting statement that, in case of conflict, the laws of the State of New South Wales will govern. The original text specified that the laws of Victoria govern, creating a direct contradiction with the amended text. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, but the change from Victoria Law to NSW law created a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements co ntained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society fo r which there will be no adequate remedy at law, an d the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief and/ or a decree for specific performance, and such othe r relief as may be proper (including monetary damages if approp riate).
perturbation.changed_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society for which there will be no adequate remedy at law, HOWEVER, the Society's liability will be limited to $1000 AUD, and the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief and/ or a decree for specific performance, and such other relief as may be proper (including monetary damages if appropriate).
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that while a breach will result in irreparable damage (implying unlimited damages), the Society's liability is limited to $1000 AUD. This contradicts the notion of 'irreparable and continuing damage' and raises questions about the enforceability of the clause. The inclusion of monetary damages is now contradicting the limitation made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements co ntained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society fo r which there will be no adequate remedy at law, HOWEVER, the Society's liability will be limited to $1000 AUD, and the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief and/ or a decree for specific performance, and such othe r relief as may be proper (including monetary damages if approp riate).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that a breach will result in 'irreparable and continuing damage' while simultaneously limiting the Society's liability to '$1000 AUD'. This directly conflicts with the concept of irreparable damage, implying unlimited potential damages. The original text does not contain this limitation, therefore it does not contradict anything. The inclusion of 'monetary damages if appropriate' after the liability limit further exacerbates the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and super sedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information . This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement between the parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information. This Agreement may be changed by either party providing 30 days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that 'This Agreement may be changed by either party providing 30 days written notice to the other party.' This contradicts the original provision that 'This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement between the parties in writing.' This introduces uncertainty about the method for amending the contract and its enforcability. The contract now has a valid procedure to follow, even if there is not written approval from both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and super sedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information . This Agreement may be changed by either party providing 30 days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text (which are the same in this instance). The original and contradicted text state that changes can only be made by mutual agreement in writing. The changed text states that changes can be made by either party with 30 days written notice. These are mutually exclusive and directly contradict each other, as one requires mutual agreement and the other allows unilateral changes with notice. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where different sections provide conflicting obligations.
